info.name: Local LLM API
info.description: Example queries for the local llama-server API wrapper with RAG, async queuing, streaming support, and multi-user auth/rate-limiting.

variable.description: Admin API key (default auth for collection)
Set automatically by async job submissions
Set automatically by Create User or List Users
Admin user_id — set automatically by List Users
Alice user_id — set automatically by List Users
Bob user_id — set automatically by List Users
Carol user_id — set automatically by List Users

request.description: Returns current user info. Runs full auth + rate limit + token limit pipeline with zero LLM inference. Used by load_test.py --mode gate.

Multimodal chat with an image URL. Requires a vision-capable model (e.g. Moondream2 with --mmproj). Content is an array of text and image_url parts following the OpenAI vision format.

Note: In Docker, llama-server may not have HTTPS support. Use the base64 variant below or run llama-server locally with SSL enabled.
Multimodal chat using the test image tests/354_512ctx.jpg encoded as base64. Image is placed before text in the content array (required by some vision models).
Streaming chat as Alice (60/min, 500/hr, 5000/day, 500k token limit).
Streaming chat as Bob (30/min, 200/hr, 2000/day, 100k token limit).
Streaming chat as Carol (Power User) — 120/min, 2000/hr, unlimited tokens.
Vision query as Alice (60 rpm, 500k token limit). Uses tests/354_512ctx.jpg encoded as base64.
Vision query as Bob (30 rpm, 100k token limit). Uses tests/354_512ctx.jpg encoded as base64.
Chat as Carol (Power User) — 120/min, 2000/hr, unlimited tokens.
Vision query as Carol (120 rpm, unlimited tokens). Uses tests/354_512ctx.jpg encoded as base64.

url.raw: {{baseUrl}}/analyze?prompt=Summarize the key points&context=Machine learning is a subset of artificial intelligence that enables systems to learn from data. It uses algorithms to identify patterns and make decisions with minimal human intervention. Common approaches include supervised learning, unsupervised learning, and reinforcement learning.

query.value: Machine learning is a subset of artificial intelligence that enables systems to learn from data. It uses algorithms to identify patterns and make decisions with minimal human intervention. Common approaches include supervised learning, unsupervised learning, and reinforcement learning.

url.raw: {{baseUrl}}/analyze?prompt=What are the benefits of test-driven development?

query.value: What are the benefits of test-driven development?

body.raw:
{
    "query": "Summarize the O-1A criteria",
    "collection": "visa-docs",
    "system_prompt": "You are an immigration lawyer. Answer based only on the provided context. Be precise and cite specific criteria.",
    "temperature": 0.3,
    "max_tokens": 256
}

item.name: Billing & Usage
item.description: Self-service endpoints for viewing your own token usage, limits, and billing summary. Available to all authenticated users.

request.description: View your own token usage across all models.
View your own token usage for a specific model. Replace 'default' with MODEL_NAME if configured.
View your token limits and remaining budget across all models. Shows remaining_tokens and usage_percent (null if unlimited).
Full billing summary: usage per model, limits with remaining budget, and multi-tier rate limit status (per-minute, per-hour, per-day limits and current counts).
Alice's billing summary (60/min, 500/hr, 5000/day, 500k token limit).
Bob's billing summary (30/min, 200/hr, 2000/day, 100k token limit).
Carol's billing summary (120/min, 2000/hr, unlimited/day, unlimited tokens).

query.description: Number of entries to return (default 50, max 100)

request.description: View your recent API call history with per-request token usage. Returns most recent calls first. Capped at 1000 entries per user.

query.description: Number of entries to return (default 50, max 100)

request.description: Alice's recent API call history (60/min, 500/hr, 5000/day, 500k token limit).

query.description: Number of entries to return (default 50, max 100)

request.description: Bob's recent API call history (30/min, 200/hr, 2000/day, 100k token limit).

query.description: Number of entries to return (default 50, max 100)

request.description: Carol's recent API call history (120/min, 2000/hr, unlimited tokens).

item.name: Admin
item.description: User management, API key management, rate limits, token limits, and usage tracking. All endpoints require admin privileges.

request.description: Lists all users. Test script auto-populates userIdAdmin, userIdAlice, userIdBob, userIdCarol variables.
Requires running List Users first to populate userIdAlice.
Requires running List Users first to populate userIdBob.
Requires running List Users first to populate userIdCarol.
Replace 'llm-abcdefgh' with the key_prefix returned from Create API Key.
Requires running List Users first to populate userIdAlice.
Set to 0 to remove limit (unlimited). Replace 'default' with MODEL_NAME if configured.

Set per-minute, per-hour, and per-day rate limits. All fields are optional. Defaults: minute=60 (DEFAULT_RATE_LIMIT), hour=0 (unlimited), day=0 (unlimited). Setting hour/day to 0 removes that tier (unlimited).
Returns per-model token usage breakdown (prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, total_tokens).
Requires running List Users first to populate userIdAlice.
Requires running List Users first to populate userIdBob.
Requires running List Users first to populate userIdCarol.
View a user's API call history with per-request token usage. Returns most recent calls first.
